Scenario Description: Successful voice enrollment with clear audio
Given:
A new user wants to enroll their voice template
AND the environment is quiet (noise <30dB)
AND the user speaks clearly in their native language
When:
The user speaks the enrollment phrase 3 times
AND each sample is 3-5 seconds duration
AND the audio quality is >85/100
Then:
The voice template should be successfully created
AND the enrollment confidence should be ≥95%
AND the template size should be <2KB
AND the process should complete in <10 seconds
Test Data: Spanish phrase: 'Mi voz es mi contraseña digital'

Scenario Description: Successful voice verification for enrolled user
Given:
An enrolled user with valid voice template
AND the user is in a standard office environment
AND background noise is <40dB
When:
The user speaks their verification phrase once
AND the audio sample is 3-5 seconds
AND the spoken phrase matches enrollment phrase
Then:
The verification should succeed
AND the match confidence should be ≥90%
AND the response time should be <3 seconds
AND the verification event should be logged

Given:
An attacker has a high-quality recording of enrolled user's voice
AND the recording is played through speakers
AND the recording quality is >80/100
When:
The attacker attempts to verify using the recorded audio
AND the audio is played at normal speaking volume
AND the recording includes the correct verification phrase
Then:
The system should detect the replay attack
AND reject the verification with confidence ≥95%
AND return error code 'REPLAY_ATTACK_DETECTED'
AND the attack should be detected within 3 seconds

Given:
An attacker uses advanced TTS software to generate speech
AND the TTS voice mimics the enrolled user
AND the generated audio includes correct phrase
When:
The attacker attempts verification using TTS-generated audio
AND the TTS quality is commercial-grade
AND the speech pattern follows natural cadence
Then:
The system should identify the synthetic speech
AND reject the verification with confidence ≥90%
AND return error code 'SYNTHETIC_VOICE_DETECTED'
AND flag the attempt for security review

Given:
The voice verification system is under normal load
AND 50 concurrent users are being processed
AND the server resources are at 60% capacity
When:
A user initiates voice verification
AND provides a clear 4-second voice sample
AND the sample meets quality thresholds
Then:
The verification should complete within 3 seconds
AND the accuracy should not degrade
AND the confidence level should remain ≥90%
AND system resources should not exceed 80%
Test Data: Clear voice sample with good SNR

Given:
An enrolled user with Portuguese language voice template
AND the user speaks with Brazilian Portuguese accent
AND the enrollment was done with Portuguese phrases
When:
The user provides verification phrase in Portuguese
AND speaks with natural Brazilian accent
AND the phrase is 'Minha voz é minha senha digital'
Then:
The verification should succeed with ≥88% accuracy
AND language detection should identify Portuguese
AND accent variation should not affect accuracy
AND processing time should remain <4 seconds

Scenario Description: Voice verification with poor audio quality
Given:
A user attempts verification with compromised audio
AND the environment has significant background noise
AND the microphone quality is below average
When:
The user speaks the verification phrase
AND background noise is 50-60dB
AND the microphone introduces distortion
Then:
The system should either request a retry with better conditions
OR attempt verification with reduced confidence threshold
AND provide clear feedback about audio quality
AND not store poor-quality templates

Given:
An attacker uses AI voice conversion technology
AND converts their voice to match enrolled user
AND the conversion quality is high (>85% similarity)
When:
The attacker attempts verification using converted voice
AND the converted sample includes correct phrase
AND the attack uses recent neural voice conversion
Then:
The system should detect voice conversion artifacts
AND reject with confidence ≥80%
AND return error code 'VOICE_CONVERSION_DETECTED'
AND alert security team for investigation

Given:
A user's first verification attempt fails due to ambient noise
AND the user is in a busy environment
AND the system detects poor audio conditions
When:
The system prompts user to try again
AND user moves to quieter location
AND user provides second voice sample
Then:
The retry mechanism should work seamlessly
AND provide helpful guidance for better audio
AND allow up to 3 attempts before lockout
AND track retry reasons for analytics
Test Data: Multiple voice samples with varying quality
Acceptance Criteria: Clear retry guidance with success on retry

Given:
A user is enrolled with both voice and face biometrics
AND both modalities are required for verification
AND user provides face scan first
When:
User provides voice sample after successful face verification
AND both biometric samples meet quality thresholds
AND samples are provided within 30-second window
Then:
The multi-modal verification should succeed
AND combined confidence should be ≥98%
AND voice verification should complete within 4 seconds
AND the session should be linked securely
Device Requirements: Smartphone with front camera and microphone

Given:
A European user wants to enroll voice biometrics
AND GDPR compliance is mandatory
AND user must provide explicit consent
When:
The user provides explicit consent for voice processing
AND completes voice enrollment
AND requests data export after 30 days
Then:
The system should store only encrypted voice templates
AND provide complete data export in machine-readable format
AND allow template deletion upon user request
AND maintain audit log of all processing

Given:
The voice verification system receives 500 concurrent requests
AND all requests are legitimate verification attempts
AND the system is at 90% normal capacity
When:
Users attempt voice verification simultaneously
AND each provides 4-second voice samples
AND samples are of varying audio quality
Then:
The system should maintain ≥95% accuracy under load
AND 95% of verifications should complete within 5 seconds
AND no verifications should timeout or fail due to load
AND system should gracefully handle queue management

Scenario Description: Voice verification for users with speech impediments
Given:
A user with mild speech impediment enrolls voice template
AND the impediment affects pronunciation but not vocal characteristics
AND user speaks slowly and clearly
When:
The user attempts verification with consistent speech pattern
AND speaks at slower pace than average
AND pronunciation matches enrollment pattern
Then:
The system should accommodate speech variations
AND verification should succeed with ≥85% accuracy
AND processing time may be extended to 6 seconds
AND provide patient user guidance
Test Data: Speech samples with consistent impediment patterns

Given:
A legitimate user starts voice verification
AND an attacker attempts to intercept the session
AND the session includes secure tokens
When:
An attacker tries to replay session tokens
AND attempts to substitute voice sample mid-verification
AND uses man-in-the-middle attack techniques
Then:
The system should detect session tampering
AND invalidate compromised sessions immediately
AND require fresh authentication start
AND log security incident for investigation
Test Data: Encrypted session tokens and voice samples

Given:
A user is mid-way through voice verification
AND the network connection is interrupted
AND 60% of voice sample has been transmitted
When:
The network connection is restored after 5 seconds
AND user's session is still valid
AND remaining voice data needs transmission
Then:
The system should resume verification seamlessly
AND not require user to restart the process
AND maintain session state during interruption
AND complete verification within extended timeout
